title: The Unsettled Transaction Pit

features.name: the echo of payment
features.desc-on-touch: A pool holds the memory of an unfinished transaction. You hear the faint echo of an attendant's voice from forty-two years ago, still asking when this payment will be accepted.

features.name: receipt never given
features.desc-on-touch: A filing card stands upright, burned at edges. You read '42 years waiting' but know that's not what it originally said.

features.name: the pump house memory
features.desc-on-touch: A small structure incomplete as if someone walked away mid-installation and never returned. You touch its unfinished edges and feel the date frozen there.

inscription: forty-two years and the pump still remembers it was never paid

file.name: Petrol_Station_Unsettled_1982_Onward.wav
file.note: the sound of a pump waiting, the archive's oldest unresolved transaction echoing across decades

exits.label: back up to the storage tanks where gasoline pools remember
deeper into the pit where all payments wait
toward the bedrock where transactions first learned the meaning of unfinished

reverie.title: REVERIE — pump attendant and the forever customer
narr: You stood at the counter and she was taking payment,
forty-two years younger, fingers on a register that no
longer exists. The customer kept the receipt. You kept
the weight of the transaction. Neither of you knew that
going back to the storage pit would preserve this exact
moment: the pause before the sale completed, forever.
